tEn: wav2vec 2.0: A Framework for Self-Supervised Learning of Speech Representations
tZh: wav2vec 2.0：一种自监督学习语音表示的框架
sEn: wav2vec 2.0 outperforms semi-supervised methods by learning from raw audio alone, achieving state-of-the-art results with minimal labeled data.
sZh: wav2vec 2.0 仅通过原始音频学习，在少量标注数据上超越半监督方法，实现最先进结果。
absEn: We show for the first time that learning powerful representations from speech audio alone followed by fine-tuning on transcribed speech can outperform the best semi-supervised methods while being conceptually simpler. wav2vec 2.0 masks the speech input in the latent space and solves a contrastive task defined over a quantization of the latent representations which are jointly learned. Experiments using all labeled data of Librispeech achieve 1.8/3.3 WER on the clean/other test sets. When lowering the amount of labeled data to one hour, wav2vec 2.0 outperforms the previous state of the art on the 100 hour subset while using 100 times less labeled data. Using just ten minutes of labeled data and pre-training on 53k hours of unlabeled data still achieves 4.8/8.2 WER. This demonstrates the feasibility of speech recognition with limited amounts of labeled data.
absZh: 我们首次证明，仅从语音音频中学习强大的表示，然后对转录语音进行微调，可以在概念上更简单的同时，超越最佳半监督方法。wav2vec 2.0 在潜在空间中屏蔽语音输入，并解决一个基于联合学习的潜在表示量化的对比任务。使用 Librispeech 的所有标注数据进行实验，在干净/其他测试集上分别实现了 1.8/3.3 的词错误率（WER）。当将标注数据量减少到一小时时，wav2vec 2.0 在 100 小时子集上超越了先前的最优结果，同时使用的标注数据量减少了 100 倍。仅使用十分钟的标注数据和 53k 小时的无标注数据进行预训练，仍然实现了 4.8/8.2 的 WER。这证明了在有限标注数据下进行语音识别的可行性。

contrib.en: Proposes wav2vec 2.0, a self-supervised framework for learning speech representations from raw audio.
contrib.zh: 提出 wav2vec 2.0，一种从原始音频中学习语音表示的自监督框架。

contrib.en: Jointly learns discrete speech units and contextualized representations via a contrastive task.
contrib.zh: 通过对比任务联合学习离散语音单元和上下文表示。

contrib.en: Achieves state-of-the-art results on Librispeech with only 10 minutes of labeled data.
contrib.zh: 仅用10分钟标注数据即在Librispeech上达到最优结果。

contrib.en: Outperforms previous semi-supervised methods while being conceptually simpler.
contrib.zh: 在概念上更简单的同时优于之前的半监督方法。

contrib.en: Demonstrates feasibility of ultra-low resource speech recognition with 53k hours of unlabeled pre-training.
contrib.zh: 展示了使用53k小时无标注预训练实现超低资源语音识别的可行性。

contrib.en: Sets new state of the art on TIMIT phoneme recognition and full Librispeech benchmark.
contrib.zh: 在TIMIT音素识别和完整Librispeech基准上创下新纪录。

limits.en: Evaluation limited to English speech datasets (Librispeech, TIMIT, LibriVox).
limits.zh: 评估仅限于英语语音数据集（Librispeech、TIMIT、LibriVox）。

limits.en: Requires large amounts of unlabeled data (53k hours) for best performance.
limits.zh: 需要大量无标注数据（53k小时）才能达到最佳性能。

limits.en: Fine-tuning still needs transcribed speech; zero-shot recognition not explored.
limits.zh: 微调仍需转录语音；未探索零样本识别。

limits.en: Model size and compute requirements are high (Large model uses 128 GPUs).
limits.zh: 模型规模和算力需求高（Large模型使用128个GPU）。

limits.en: Character-based vocabulary may limit performance compared to word pieces.
limits.zh: 基于字符的词汇表可能限制性能，相比词片方法。

items.en: We show for the first time that learning powerful representations from speech audio alone followed by fine-tuning on transcribed speech can outperform the best semi-supervised methods while being conceptually simpler. wav2vec 2.0 masks the speech input in the latent space and solves a contrastive task defined over a quantization of the latent representations which are jointly learned. Experiments using all labeled data of Librispeech achieve 1.8/3.3 WER on the clean/other test sets. When lowering the amount of labeled data to one hour, wav2vec 2.0 outperforms the previous state of the art on the 100 hour subset while using 100 times less labeled data. Using just ten minutes of labeled data and pre-training on 53k hours of unlabeled data still achieves 4.8/8.2 WER. This demonstrates the feasibility of speech recognition with limited amounts of labeled data.111Code and models are available at https://github.com/pytorch/fairseq
items.zh: 我们首次证明，仅从语音音频中学习强大表示，随后在有转录语音上进行微调，可以超越最佳半监督方法，同时概念上更简单。wav2vec 2.0 在潜在空间中掩蔽语音输入，并解决一个基于联合学习的潜在表示量化的对比任务。使用 Librispeech 所有标注数据的实验在 clean/other 测试集上实现了 1.8/3.3 的词错误率（WER）。当将标注数据量减少到一小时时，wav2vec 2.0 在 100 小时子集上超越了先前的最优结果，同时使用的标注数据量少了 100 倍。仅使用十分钟的标注数据和 53k 小时的无标注数据进行预训练，仍能达到 4.8/8.2 的 WER。这证明了在有限标注数据下语音识别的可行性。代码和模型可在 https://github.com/pytorch/fairseq 获取。

items.en: Neural networks benefit from large quantities of labeled training data. However, in many settings labeled data is much harder to come by than unlabeled data: current speech recognition systems require thousands of hours of transcribed speech to reach acceptable performance which is not available for the vast majority of the nearly 7,000 languages spoken worldwide [31]. Learning purely from labeled examples does not resemble language acquisition in humans: infants learn language by listening to adults around them - a process that requires learning good representations of speech.
items.zh: 神经网络受益于大量标注训练数据。然而，在许多情况下，标注数据比无标注数据更难获得：当前的语音识别系统需要数千小时的转录语音才能达到可接受的性能，而对于全球近 7000 种语言中的绝大多数来说，这是不可用的 [31]。仅从标注示例学习并不类似于人类语言习得：婴儿通过听周围成年人的语言来学习语言——这一过程需要学习良好的语音表示。

items.en: In machine learning, self-supervised learning has emerged as a paradigm to learn general data representations from unlabeled examples and to fine-tune the model on labeled data. This has been particularly successful for natural language processing [43, 45, 9] and is an active research area for computer vision [20, 2, 36, 19, 6].
items.zh: 在机器学习中，自监督学习已成为一种从无标注示例中学习通用数据表示并在标注数据上微调模型的范式。这在自然语言处理中特别成功 [43, 45, 9]，并且是计算机视觉的一个活跃研究领域 [20, 2, 36, 19, 6]。

items.en: In this paper, we present a framework for self-supervised learning of representations from raw audio data. Our approach encodes speech audio via a multi-layer convolutional neural network and then masks spans of the resulting latent speech representations [26, 56], similar to masked language modeling [9]. The latent representations are fed to a Transformer network to build contextualized representations and the model is trained via a contrastive task where the true latent is to be distinguished from distractors [54, 49, 48, 28] (§ 2).
items.zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一个从原始音频数据中自监督学习表示的框架。我们的方法通过多层卷积神经网络编码语音音频，然后掩蔽所得潜在语音表示的跨度 [26, 56]，类似于掩蔽语言建模 [9]。潜在表示被馈送到 Transformer 网络以构建上下文表示，模型通过对比任务进行训练，其中需要将真实潜在表示与干扰项区分开 [54, 49, 48, 28]（§ 2）。

items.en: As part of training, we learn discrete speech units [53, 32, 7, 18] via a gumbel softmax [24, 5] to represent the latent representations in the contrastive task (Figure 1) which we find to be more effective than non-quantized targets. After pre-training on unlabeled speech, the model is fine-tuned on labeled data with a Connectionist Temporal Classification (CTC) loss [14, 4] to be used for downstream speech recognition tasks (§ 3)
items.zh: 作为训练的一部分，我们通过 Gumbel softmax [24, 5] 学习离散语音单元 [53, 32, 7, 18] 来表示对比任务中的潜在表示（图 1），我们发现这比非量化目标更有效。在无标注语音上预训练后，模型使用连接主义时间分类（CTC）损失 [14, 4] 在标注数据上进行微调，用于下游语音识别任务（§ 3）。

items.capEn: Figure 1: Illustration of our framework which jointly learns contextualized speech representations and an inventory of discretized speech units.
items.capZh: 图 1：我们的框架示意图，该框架联合学习上下文语音表示和离散化语音单元的库存。

items.en: Previous work learned a quantization of the data followed by a contextualized representations with a self-attention model [5, 4], whereas our approach solves both problems end-to-end. Masking parts of the input with Transformer networks for speech has been explored [4, 26], but prior work relies either on a two-step pipeline or their model is trained by reconstructing the filter bank input features. Other related work includes learning representations from auto-encoding the input data [52, 11] or directly predicting future timesteps [8].
items.zh: 先前的工作先学习数据量化，然后通过自注意力模型学习上下文表示 [5, 4]，而我们的方法端到端地解决这两个问题。使用 Transformer 网络掩蔽语音输入的部分已被探索 [4, 26]，但先前的工作要么依赖于两步流水线，要么通过重构滤波器组输入特征来训练模型。其他相关工作包括通过自编码输入数据 [52, 11] 或直接预测未来时间步 [8] 来学习表示。

items.en: Our results show that jointly learning discrete speech units with contextualized representations achieves substantially better results than fixed units learned in a prior step [4]. We also demonstrate the feasibility of ultra-low resource speech recognition: when using only 10 minutes of labeled data, our approach achieves word error rate (WER) 4.8/8.2 on the clean/other test sets of Librispeech. We set a new state of the art on TIMIT phoneme recognition as well as the 100 hour clean subset of Librispeech. Moreover, when we lower the amount of labeled data to just one hour, we still outperform the previous state of the art self-training method of [42] while using 100 times less labeled data and the same amount of unlabeled data. When we use all 960 hours of labeled data from Librispeech, then our model achieves 1.8/3.3 WER (§ 4, § 5).
items.zh: 我们的结果表明，联合学习离散语音单元与上下文表示比在先前步骤中学习固定单元 [4] 取得了显著更好的结果。我们还证明了超低资源语音识别的可行性：当仅使用 10 分钟标注数据时，我们的方法在 Librispeech 的 clean/other 测试集上实现了 4.8/8.2 的词错误率（WER）。我们在 TIMIT 音素识别以及 Librispeech 的 100 小时干净子集上设立了新的最优结果。此外，当我们将标注数据量减少到仅一小时时，我们仍然优于 [42] 的先前最优自训练方法，同时使用的标注数据量少了 100 倍，且无标注数据量相同。当我们使用 Librispeech 所有 960 小时标注数据时，我们的模型实现了 1.8/3.3 的 WER（§ 4, § 5）。

items.en: Our model is composed of a multi-layer convolutional feature encoder f:𝒳↦𝒵:𝑓maps-to𝒳𝒵f:\mathcal{X}\mapsto\mathcal{Z} which takes as input raw audio 𝒳𝒳\mathcal{X} and outputs latent speech representations 𝐳1,…,𝐳Tsubscript𝐳1…subscript𝐳𝑇\mathbf{z}_{1},\dots,\mathbf{z}_{T} for T𝑇T time-steps. They are then fed to a Transformer g:𝒵↦𝒞:𝑔maps-to𝒵𝒞g:\mathcal{Z}\mapsto\mathcal{C} to build representations 𝐜1,…,𝐜Tsubscript𝐜1…subscript𝐜𝑇\mathbf{c}_{1},\dots,\mathbf{c}_{T} capturing information from the entire sequence [9, 5, 4]. The output of the feature encoder is discretized to 𝐪tsubscript𝐪𝑡\mathbf{q}_{t} with a quantization module 𝒵↦𝒬maps-to𝒵𝒬\mathcal{Z}\mapsto\mathcal{Q} to represent the targets (Figure 1) in the self-supervised objective (§ 3.2). Compared to vq-wav2vec [5], our model builds context representations over continuous speech representations and self-attention captures dependencies over the entire sequence of latent representations end-to-end.
items.zh: 我们的模型由一个多层卷积特征编码器 f: 𝒳 ↦ 𝒵 组成，该编码器以原始音频 𝒳 为输入，并输出 T 个时间步的潜在语音表示 𝐳₁, …, 𝐳_T。然后它们被馈送到 Transformer g: 𝒵 ↦ 𝒞，以构建捕获整个序列信息的表示 𝐜₁, …, 𝐜_T [9, 5, 4]。特征编码器的输出通过量化模块 𝒵 ↦ 𝒬 离散化为 𝐪_t，以表示自监督目标中的目标（图 1）（§ 3.2）。与 vq-wav2vec [5] 相比，我们的模型在连续语音表示上构建上下文表示，并且自注意力端到端地捕获整个潜在表示序列上的依赖关系。

items.en: The encoder consists of several blocks containing a temporal convolution followed by layer normalization [1] and a GELU activation function [21]. The raw waveform input to the encoder is normalized to zero mean and unit variance. The total stride of the encoder determines the number of time-steps T𝑇T which are input to the Transformer (§ 4.2).
items.zh: 编码器由多个块组成，每个块包含一个时间卷积，随后是层归一化 [1] 和 GELU 激活函数 [21]。编码器的原始波形输入被归一化为零均值和单位方差。编码器的总步幅决定了输入到 Transformer 的时间步数 T（§ 4.2）。

items.en: The output of the feature encoder is fed to a context network which follows the Transformer architecture [55, 9, 33]. Instead of fixed positional embeddings which encode absolute positional information, we use a convolutional layer similar to [37, 4, 57] which acts as relative positional embedding. We add the output of the convolution followed by a GELU to the inputs and then apply layer normalization.
items.zh: 特征编码器的输出被馈送到一个上下文网络，该网络遵循 Transformer 架构 [55, 9, 33]。我们使用类似于 [37, 4, 57] 的卷积层作为相对位置嵌入，而不是编码绝对位置信息的固定位置嵌入。我们将卷积的输出加上 GELU 后添加到输入中，然后应用层归一化。

items.en: For self-supervised training we discretize the output of the feature encoder 𝐳𝐳\mathbf{z} to a finite set of speech representations via product quantization [25]. This choice led to good results in prior work which learned discrete units in a first step followed by learning contextualized representations [5]. Product quantization amounts to choosing quantized representations from multiple codebooks and concatenating them. Given G𝐺G codebooks, or groups, with V𝑉V entries e∈ℝV×d/G𝑒superscriptℝ𝑉𝑑𝐺e\in\mathbb{R}^{V\times d/G}, we choose one entry from each codebook and concatenate the resulting vectors e1,…,eGsubscript𝑒1…subscript𝑒𝐺e_{1},\dots,e_{G} and apply a linear transformation ℝd↦ℝfmaps-tosuperscriptℝ𝑑superscriptℝ𝑓\mathbb{R}^{d}\mapsto\mathbb{R}^{f} to obtain 𝐪∈ℝf𝐪superscriptℝ𝑓\mathbf{q}\in\mathbb{R}^{f}.
items.zh: 对于自监督训练，我们通过乘积量化 [25] 将特征编码器的输出 𝐳 离散化为有限集合的语音表示。这一选择在先前的工作中取得了良好结果，该工作先学习离散单元，然后学习上下文表示 [5]。乘积量化相当于从多个码本中选择量化表示并拼接它们。给定 G 个码本（或组），每个码本有 V 个条目 e ∈ ℝ^{V×d/G}，我们从每个码本中选择一个条目，拼接得到的向量 e₁, …, e_G，并应用线性变换 ℝ^d ↦ ℝ^f 以获得 𝐪 ∈ ℝ^f。

items.en: The Gumbel softmax enables choosing discrete codebook entries in a fully differentiable way [16, 24, 35]. We use the straight-through estimator [26] and setup G𝐺G hard Gumbel softmax operations [24]. The feature encoder output 𝐳𝐳\mathbf{z} is mapped to 𝐥∈ℝG×V𝐥superscriptℝ𝐺𝑉\mathbf{l}\in\mathbb{R}^{G\times V} logits and the probabilities for choosing the v𝑣v-th codebook entry for group g𝑔g are
items.zh: Gumbel softmax 使得能够以完全可微的方式选择离散码本条目 [16, 24, 35]。我们使用直通估计器 [26] 并设置 G 个硬 Gumbel softmax 操作 [24]。特征编码器输出 𝐳 被映射到 logits 𝐥 ∈ ℝ^{G×V}，选择组 g 的第 v 个码本条目的概率为：

items.en: where τ𝜏\tau is a non-negative temperature, n=−log⁡(−log⁡(u))𝑛𝑢n=-\log(-\log(u)) and u𝑢u are uniform samples from 𝒰​(0,1)𝒰01\mathcal{U}(0,1). During the forward pass, codeword i𝑖i is chosen by i=argmaxj​pg,j𝑖subscriptargmax𝑗subscript𝑝𝑔𝑗i=\text{argmax}_{j}p_{g,j} and in the backward pass, the true gradient of the Gumbel softmax outputs is used.
items.zh: 其中 τ 是非负温度，n = -log(-log(u))，u 是从 𝒰(0,1) 均匀采样的样本。在前向传播中，通过 i = argmax_j p_{g,j} 选择码字 i，在反向传播中，使用 Gumbel softmax 输出的真实梯度。

items.en: To pre-train the model we mask a certain proportion of time steps in the latent feature encoder space (§ 3.1), similar to masked language modeling in BERT [9]. The training objective requires identifying the correct quantized latent audio representation in a set of distractors for each masked time step (§ 3.2) and the final model is fine-tuned on the labeled data (§ 3.3).
items.zh: 为了预训练模型，我们在潜在特征编码器空间中掩蔽一定比例的时间步（§ 3.1），类似于 BERT 中的掩蔽语言建模 [9]。训练目标要求为每个掩蔽时间步在一组干扰项中识别正确的量化潜在音频表示（§ 3.2），最终模型在标注数据上进行微调（§ 3.3）。

items.en: We mask a proportion of the feature encoder outputs, or time steps before feeding them to the context network and replace them with a trained feature vector shared between all masked time steps; we do not mask inputs to the quantization module. To mask the latent speech representations output by the encoder, we randomly sample without replacement a certain proportion p𝑝p of all time steps to be starting indices and then mask the subsequent M𝑀M consecutive time steps from every sampled index; spans may overlap.
items.zh: 我们在将特征编码器输出（或时间步）馈送到上下文网络之前掩蔽其中一部分，并用一个在所有掩蔽时间步之间共享的训练特征向量替换它们；我们不掩蔽量化模块的输入。为了掩蔽编码器输出的潜在语音表示，我们无放回地随机采样一定比例 p 的所有时间步作为起始索引，然后从每个采样索引开始掩蔽后续 M 个连续时间步；跨度可能重叠。

items.en: During pre-training, we learn representations of speech audio by solving a contrastive task ℒmsubscriptℒ𝑚\mathcal{L}_{m}{} which requires to identify the true quantized latent speech representation for a masked time step within a set of distractors. This is augmented by a codebook diversity loss ℒdsubscriptℒ𝑑\mathcal{L}_{d}{} to encourage the model to use the codebook entries equally often.
items.zh: 在预训练期间，我们通过解决对比任务 ℒ_m 来学习语音音频的表示，该任务要求在一组干扰项中识别掩蔽时间步的真实量化潜在语音表示。这通过码本多样性损失 ℒ_d 进行增强，以鼓励模型均匀地使用码本条目。

items.en: where α𝛼\alpha is a tuned hyperparameter.
items.zh: 其中 α 是调优的超参数。

items.en: Given context network output 𝐜tsubscript𝐜𝑡\mathbf{c}_{t} centered over masked time step t𝑡t, the model needs to identify the true quantized latent speech representation 𝐪tsubscript𝐪𝑡\mathbf{q}_{t} in a set of K+1𝐾1K+1 quantized candidate representations 𝐪~∈𝐐t~𝐪subscript𝐐𝑡\mathbf{\tilde{q}}\in\mathbf{Q}_{t} which includes 𝐪tsubscript𝐪𝑡\mathbf{q}_{t} and K𝐾K distractors [23, 54]. Distractors are uniformly sampled from other masked time steps of the same utterance. The loss is defined as
items.zh: 给定上下文网络输出 𝐜_t（以掩蔽时间步 t 为中心），模型需要在一组 K+1 个量化候选表示 𝐪̃ ∈ 𝐐_t（包括 𝐪_t 和 K 个干扰项）中识别真实的量化潜在语音表示 𝐪_t [23, 54]。干扰项从同一话语的其他掩蔽时间步中均匀采样。损失定义为：

items.en: where we compute the cosine similarity s​i​m​(𝐚,𝐛)=𝐚T​𝐛/‖𝐚‖​‖𝐛‖𝑠𝑖𝑚𝐚𝐛superscript𝐚𝑇𝐛norm𝐚norm𝐛sim(\mathbf{a},\mathbf{b})=\mathbf{a}^{T}\mathbf{b}/\|\mathbf{a}\|\|\mathbf{b}\| between context representations and quantized latent speech representations [19, 6].
items.zh: 其中我们计算上下文表示与量化潜在语音表示之间的余弦相似度 sim(𝐚, 𝐛) = 𝐚^T 𝐛 / (‖𝐚‖ ‖𝐛‖) [19, 6]。

items.en: The contrastive task depends on the codebook to represent both positive and negative examples and the diversity loss ℒdsubscriptℒ𝑑\mathcal{L}_{d} is designed to increase the use of the quantized codebook representations [10]. We encourage the equal use of the V𝑉V entries in each of the G𝐺G codebooks by maximizing the entropy of the averaged softmax distribution 𝐥𝐥\mathbf{l} over the codebook entries for each codebook p¯gsubscript¯𝑝𝑔\bar{p}_{g} across a batch of utterances; the softmax disribution does not contain the gumbel noise nor a temperature:222Our implementation maximizes perplexity G​V−∑g=1Gexp⁡(−∑v=1Vpg​v​log⁡pg​v)G​V𝐺𝑉superscriptsubscript𝑔1𝐺superscriptsubscript𝑣1𝑉subscript𝑝𝑔𝑣subscript𝑝𝑔𝑣𝐺𝑉\frac{GV-\sum_{g=1}^{G}\exp(-\sum_{v=1}^{V}p_{gv}\log{p_{gv}})}{GV} which is equivalent.
items.zh: 对比任务依赖码本表示正负样本，多样性损失 ℒd 旨在增加量化码本表示的使用 [10]。我们通过最大化每个码本在批话语中平均 softmax 分布 p̄g 的熵，鼓励 G 个码本中每个码本的 V 个条目被平等使用；softmax 分布不包含 Gumbel 噪声或温度：222我们的实现最大化困惑度 GV - ∑_{g=1}^{G} exp(-∑_{v=1}^{V} p_{gv} log p_{gv}) / GV，这是等价的。

items.en: Pre-trained models are fine-tuned for speech recognition by adding a randomly initialized linear projection on top of the context network into C𝐶C classes representing the vocabulary of the task [4]. For Librispeech, we have 29 tokens for character targets plus a word boundary token. Models are optimized by minimizing a CTC loss [14] and we apply a modified version of SpecAugment [41] by masking to time-steps and channels during training which delays overfitting and significantly improves the final error rates, especially on the Libri-light subsets with few labeled examples.
items.zh: 预训练模型通过随机初始化的线性投影在上下文网络顶部添加 C 类（代表任务词汇）进行微调，用于语音识别 [4]。对于 Librispeech，我们有 29 个字符目标 token 加上一个词边界 token。模型通过最小化 CTC 损失 [14] 进行优化，并应用修改版的 SpecAugment [41]，在训练期间对时间步和通道进行掩码，这延迟了过拟合并显著改善了最终错误率，尤其是在标记样本较少的 Libri-light 子集上。

items.en: As unlabeled data we consider the Librispeech corpus [40] without transcriptions containing 960 hours of audio (LS-960) or the audio data from LibriVox (LV-60k). For the latter we follow the pre-processing of [27] resulting in 53.2k hours of audio. We fine-tune on five labeled data settings: 960 hours of transcribed Librispeech, the train-clean-100 subset comprising 100 hours (100 hours labeled), as well as the Libri-light limited resource training subsets originally extracted from Librispeech, these are train-10h (10 hours labeled), train-1h (1 hour labeled), train-10min (10 min labeled). We follow the evaluation protocol of Libri-light for these splits and evaluate on the standard Librispech dev-other/clean and test-clean/other sets.
items.zh: 作为无标签数据，我们考虑 Librispeech 语料库 [40] 中无转录的 960 小时音频（LS-960）或来自 LibriVox 的音频数据（LV-60k）。对于后者，我们遵循 [27] 的预处理，得到 53.2k 小时音频。我们在五个标记数据设置上进行微调：960 小时转录的 Librispeech、包含 100 小时的 train-clean-100 子集（100 小时标记），以及最初从 Librispeech 提取的 Libri-light 有限资源训练子集，即 train-10h（10 小时标记）、train-1h（1 小时标记）、train-10min（10 分钟标记）。我们遵循 Libri-light 对这些分区的评估协议，并在标准 Librispeech dev-other/clean 和 test-clean/other 集上评估。

items.en: We fine-tune the pre-trained models for phoneme recognition on the TIMIT dataset [13]. It contains five hours of audio recordings with detailed phoneme labels. We use the standard train, dev and test split and follow the standard protocol of collapsing phone labels to 39 classes.
items.zh: 我们在 TIMIT 数据集 [13] 上微调预训练模型用于音素识别。它包含五小时带有详细音素标签的音频录音。我们使用标准的训练、开发和测试划分，并遵循将音素标签折叠为 39 类的标准协议。

items.en: Models are implemented in fairseq [39]. For masking, we sample p=0.065𝑝0.065p=0.065 of all time-steps to be starting indices and mask the subsequent M=10𝑀10M=10 time-steps. This results in approximately 49% of all time steps to be masked with a mean span length of 14.7, or 299ms (see Appendix A for more details on masking).
items.zh: 模型在 fairseq [39] 中实现。对于掩码，我们采样 p=0.065 的所有时间步作为起始索引，并掩码后续 M=10 个时间步。这导致大约 49% 的时间步被掩码，平均跨度长度为 14.7，即 299ms（更多掩码细节见附录 A）。

items.en: The feature encoder contains seven blocks and the temporal convolutions in each block have 512 channels with strides (5,2,2,2,2,2,2) and kernel widths (10,3,3,3,3,2,2). This results in an encoder output frequency of 49 hz with a stride of about 20ms between each sample, and a receptive field of 400 input samples or 25ms of audio. The convolutional layer modeling relative positional embeddings has kernel size 128 and 16 groups.
items.zh: 特征编码器包含七个块，每个块中的时间卷积有 512 个通道，步幅为 (5,2,2,2,2,2,2)，核宽度为 (10,3,3,3,3,2,2)。这导致编码器输出频率为 49 Hz，每个样本之间的步幅约为 20ms，感受野为 400 个输入样本或 25ms 音频。建模相对位置嵌入的卷积层核大小为 128，有 16 个组。

items.en: We experiment with two model configurations which use the same encoder architecture but differ in the Transformer setup: Base contains 12 transformer blocks, model dimension 768, inner dimension (FFN) 3,072 and 8 attention heads. Batches are built by cropping 250k audio samples, or 15.6sec, from each example. Crops are batched together to not exceed 1.4m samples per GPU and we train on a total of 64 V100 GPUs for 1.6 days [38]; the total batch size is 1.6h.
items.zh: 我们实验了两种模型配置，它们使用相同的编码器架构但 Transformer 设置不同：Base 包含 12 个 Transformer 块，模型维度 768，内部维度（FFN）3072，8 个注意力头。批次通过从每个示例裁剪 250k 音频样本（即 15.6 秒）构建。裁剪后的样本被批处理，每个 GPU 不超过 140 万样本，我们在总共 64 个 V100 GPU 上训练 1.6 天 [38]；总批次大小为 1.6 小时。

items.en: The Large model contains 24 transformer blocks with model dimension 1,024, inner dimension 4,096 and 16 attention heads. We crop 320k audio samples, or 20sec, with a limit of 1.2m samples per GPU and train on 128 V100 GPUs over 2.3 days for Librispeech and 5.2 days for LibriVox; the total batch size is 2.7h. We use dropout 0.1 in the Transformer, at the output of the feature encoder and the input to the quantization module. Layers are dropped at a rate of 0.05 for Base and 0.2 for Large [22, 12]; there is no layer drop for LV-60k.
items.zh: Large 模型包含 24 个 Transformer 块，模型维度 1024，内部维度 4096，16 个注意力头。我们裁剪 320k 音频样本（即 20 秒），每个 GPU 限制为 120 万样本，在 128 个 V100 GPU 上训练 Librispeech 2.3 天，LibriVox 5.2 天；总批次大小为 2.7 小时。我们在 Transformer 中、特征编码器输出和量化模块输入处使用 dropout 0.1。Base 的层丢弃率为 0.05，Large 为 0.2 [22, 12]；LV-60k 没有层丢弃。

items.en: We optimize with Adam [29], warming up the learning rate for the first 8% of updates to a peak of 5×10−45E-45\text{\times}{10}^{-4} for Base and 3×10−43E-43\text{\times}{10}^{-4} for Large, and then linearly decay it. Large trains for 250k updates, Base for 400k updates, and Large on LV-60k for 600k updates. We use weight α=0.1𝛼0.1\alpha=0.1 for the diversity loss Equation 2. For the quantization module we use G=2𝐺2G=2 and V=320𝑉320V=320 for both models, resulting in a theoretical maximum of 102.4k codewords. Entries are of size d/G=128𝑑𝐺128d/G=128 for Base amd d/G=384𝑑𝐺384d/G=384 for Large. The Gumbel softmax temperature τ𝜏\tau is annealed from 2 to a minimum of 0.5 for Base and 0.1 for Large by a factor of 0.999995 at every update. The temperature in the contrastive loss (Equation 3) is set to κ=0.1𝜅0.1\kappa=0.1. For the smaller Librispeech dataset, we regularize the model by applying an L2 penalty to the activations of the final layer of the feature encoder and scale down the gradients for the encoder by a factor of 10. We also use a slightly different encoder architecture where we do not use layer normalization, and instead of normalizing the raw waveform, the output of the first encoder layer is normalized. In the contrastive loss we use K=100𝐾100K=100 distractors. We choose the training checkpoint with the lowest ℒmsubscriptℒ𝑚\mathcal{L}_{m}{} on the validation set.
items.zh: 我们使用 Adam [29] 优化，在前 8% 的更新中将学习率预热到峰值：Base 为 5×10^{-4}，Large 为 3×10^{-4}，然后线性衰减。Large 训练 250k 更新，Base 训练 400k 更新，LV-60k 上的 Large 训练 600k 更新。我们对多样性损失（公式 2）使用权重 α=0.1。对于量化模块，两种模型均使用 G=2 和 V=320，理论最大码字数为 102.4k。条目大小为 Base 的 d/G=128 和 Large 的 d/G=384。Gumbel softmax 温度 τ 从 2 退火到最小值：Base 为 0.5，Large 为 0.1，每次更新衰减因子为 0.999995。对比损失（公式 3）中的温度设置为 κ=0.1。对于较小的 Librispeech 数据集，我们通过对特征编码器最后一层的激活施加 L2 惩罚来正则化模型，并将编码器的梯度缩小 10 倍。我们还使用略有不同的编码器架构，不使用层归一化，而是对原始波形进行归一化，对第一个编码器层的输出进行归一化。在对比损失中，我们使用 K=100 个干扰项。我们选择验证集上 ℒm 最低的训练检查点。

items.en: After pre-training we fine-tune the learned representations on labeled data and add a randomly initialized output layer on top of the Transformer to predict characters (Librispeech/Libri-light) or phonemes (TIMIT). For Libri-light, we train three seeds with two different learning rates (2e-5 and 3e-5) for all subsets and choose the configuration with lowest WER on dev-other subset decoded with the official 4-gram language model (LM) with beam 50 and fixed model weights (LM weight 2, word insertion penalty -1). For Base on the labeled 960h subset we use a learning rate of 1e-4.
items.zh: 预训练后，我们在标记数据上微调学习到的表示，并在 Transformer 顶部添加随机初始化的输出层来预测字符（Librispeech/Libri-light）或音素（TIMIT）。对于 Libri-light，我们使用两种不同的学习率（2e-5 和 3e-5）对所有子集训练三个种子，并选择在 dev-other 子集上使用官方 4-gram 语言模型（LM）解码（波束 50，固定模型权重，LM 权重 2，词插入惩罚 -1）时 WER 最低的配置。对于标记的 960h 子集上的 Base，我们使用学习率 1e-4。

items.en: We optimize with Adam and a tri-state rate schedule where the learning rate is warmed up for the first 10% of updates, held constant for the next 40% and then linearly decayed for the remainder. Base uses a batch size of 3.2m samples per GPU and we fine-tune on 8 GPUs, giving a total batch size of 1,600sec. Large batches 1.28m samples on each GPU and we fine-tune on 24 GPUs, resulting in an effective batch size of 1,920sec. For the first 10k updates only the output classifier is trained, after which the Transformer is also updated. The feature encoder is not trained during fine-tuning. We mask the feature encoder representations with a strategy similar to SpecAugment [41] detailed in Appendix B.
items.zh: 我们使用 Adam 和三阶段学习率调度进行优化，其中学习率在前 10% 的更新中预热，接下来 40% 保持恒定，然后线性衰减。Base 使用每个 GPU 320 万样本的批次大小，在 8 个 GPU 上微调，总批次大小为 1600 秒。Large 每个 GPU 批次 128 万样本，在 24 个 GPU 上微调，有效批次大小为 1920 秒。在前 10k 更新中，只训练输出分类器，之后 Transformer 也进行更新。微调期间不训练特征编码器。我们使用类似于 SpecAugment [41] 的策略对特征编码器表示进行掩码，详见附录 B。

items.en: We consider two types of language models (LM): a 4-gram model and a Transformer [3] trained on the Librispeech LM corpus. The Transformer LM is identical to [51] and contains 20 blocks, model dimension 1,280, inner dimension 6,144 and 16 attention heads. We tune the weights of the language model (interval [0,5]05[0,5]) and a word insertion penalty ([−5,5]55[-5,5]) via Bayesian optimization333https://github.com/facebook/Ax: we run 128 trials with beam 500 for the 4-gram LM and beam 50 for the Transformer LM and choose the best set of weights according to performance on dev-other. Test performance is measured with beam 1,500 for the n-gram LM and beam 500 for the Transformer LM. We use the beam search decoder of [44].
items.zh: 我们考虑两种类型的语言模型（LM）：一个 4-gram 模型和一个在 Librispeech LM 语料库上训练的 Transformer [3]。Transformer LM 与 [51] 相同，包含 20 个块，模型维度 1280，内部维度 6144，16 个注意力头。我们通过贝叶斯优化调整语言模型的权重（区间 [0,5]）和词插入惩罚（[-5,5]）：对于 4-gram LM，我们运行 128 次试验，波束为 500；对于 Transformer LM，波束为 50，并根据 dev-other 上的性能选择最佳权重集。测试性能使用 n-gram LM 的波束 1500 和 Transformer LM 的波束 500 测量。我们使用 [44] 的波束搜索解码器。

items.en: We first evaluate our pre-trained models in settings where the amount of labeled data is limited to get a sense of how the representations learned on unlabeled data can improve low resource settings. If a pre-trained model captures the structure of speech, then it should require few labeled examples to fine-tune it for speech recognition. The models are pre-trained on the audio data of either Librispeech (LS-960) or LibriVox (LV-60k) and most results are obtained by decoding with a Transformer language model (Transf.); Appendix C shows results with no language model at all as well as with an n-gram language model.
items.zh: 我们首先在标记数据量有限的设置中评估预训练模型，以了解无标签数据学习到的表示如何改善低资源设置。如果预训练模型捕捉到了语音结构，那么它应该只需要少量标记示例即可微调用于语音识别。模型在 Librispeech（LS-960）或 LibriVox（LV-60k）的音频数据上预训练，大多数结果通过使用 Transformer 语言模型（Transf.）解码获得；附录 C 显示了不使用语言模型以及使用 n-gram 语言模型的结果。

items.en: The Large model pre-trained on LV-60k and fine-tuned on only 10 minutes of labeled data achieves a word error rate of 5.2/8.6 on the Librispeech clean/other test sets. Ten minutes of labeled data corresponds to just 48 recordings with an average length of 12.5 seconds. This demonstrates that ultra-low resource speech recognition is possible with self-supervised learning on unlabeled data. Our approach of jointly learning discrete units and contextualized representations clearly improves over previous work which learned quantized audio units in a separate step [4], reducing WER by a about a third.
items.zh: 在 LV-60k 上预训练并在仅 10 分钟标记数据上微调的 Large 模型，在 Librispeech clean/other 测试集上实现了 5.2/8.6 的词错误率。十分钟标记数据对应于仅 48 个平均长度为 12.5 秒的录音。这表明通过无标签数据的自监督学习可以实现超低资源语音识别。我们的联合学习离散单元和上下文表示的方法明显优于先前在单独步骤中学习量化音频单元的工作 [4]，将 WER 降低了约三分之一。

items.en: A recent iterative self-training approach [42] represents the state of the art on the clean 100 hour subset of Librispeech but it requires multiple iterations of labeling, filtering, and re-training. Our approach is simpler: we pre-train on the unlabeled data and fine-tune on the labeled data. On the 100 hour subset of Librispeech, their method achieves WER 4.2/8.6 on test-clean/other which compares to WER 2.3/5.0 with the Large model in a like for like setup, a relative WER reduction of 45%/42%.
items.zh: 最近的迭代自训练方法 [42] 代表了 Librispeech clean 100 小时子集上的最新技术，但它需要多轮标记、过滤和再训练。我们的方法更简单：我们在无标签数据上预训练，在标记数据上微调。在 Librispeech 的 100 小时子集上，他们的方法在 test-clean/other 上达到 WER 4.2/8.6，而在类似设置下，Large 模型达到 WER 2.3/5.0，相对 WER 降低 45%/42%。

items.en: When the Large model uses an order of magnitude less labeled data (10h labeled), then it still achieves WER 3.2/6.1, an error reduction of 24%/29% relative to iterative self-training. Using only a single hour of labeled data, the same model achieves WER 3.9/7.6 which improves on both test-clean and test-other by 7%/12% - with two orders of magnitude less labeled data. We note that the Libri-light data splits contain both clean and noisy data leading to better accuracy on test-other compared to test-clean. Increasing model size reduces WER on all setups with the largest improvements on test-other (Base vs. Large both on LS-960) and increasing the amount of unlabeled training data also leads to large improvements (Large LS-960 vs. LV-60k).
items.zh: 当 Large 模型使用少一个数量级的标记数据（10h 标记）时，它仍然达到 WER 3.2/6.1，相对于迭代自训练错误率降低 24%/29%。仅使用一小时的标记数据，同一模型达到 WER 3.9/7.6，在 test-clean 和 test-other 上分别改进 7%/12%——使用的标记数据少两个数量级。我们注意到 Libri-light 数据划分包含干净和噪声数据，导致 test-other 上的准确率高于 test-clean。增加模型大小在所有设置上降低了 WER，在 test-other 上改进最大（Base 与 Large 均在 LS-960 上），增加无标签训练数据量也带来了大幅改进（Large LS-960 与 LV-60k）。

full.sec: 5.2 High-Resource Labeled Data Evaluation on Librispeech

items.en: In this section we evaluate the performance when large quantities of labeled speech are available to assess the effectiveness of our approach in a high resource setup. Specifically, we fine-tune the same models as before on the full 960 hours of labeled Librispeech: Base and Large pre-trained on LS-960 as well as Large pre-trained on LV-60k.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们评估当大量标记语音可用时的性能，以评估我们的方法在高资源设置中的有效性。具体来说，我们在全部 960 小时标记 Librispeech 上微调相同的模型：在 LS-960 上预训练的 Base 和 Large，以及在 LV-60k 上预训练的 Large。

items.en: Note that the vocabulary of our acoustic model (characters) does not match the vocabulary of the LM (words) which delays feedback from the LM and is likely to be detrimental. Most recent work [51, 58, 17, 42] uses the better performing word pieces [50] for both models. Moreover, our result is achieved without any data balancing such as [42]. Finally, self-training is likely complimentary to pre-training and their combination may yield even better results. Appendix E presents a detailed error analysis of our pre-trained models in various labeled data setups.
items.zh: 注意，我们的声学模型词汇（字符）与 LM 词汇（词）不匹配，这延迟了来自 LM 的反馈，可能是有害的。大多数最近的工作 [51, 58, 17, 42] 对两个模型都使用性能更好的词片 [50]。此外，我们的结果是在没有任何数据平衡（如 [42]）的情况下实现的。最后，自训练很可能与预训练互补，它们的结合可能产生更好的结果。附录 E 展示了我们的预训练模型在各种标记数据设置中的详细错误分析。

items.en: Next, we evaluate accuracy on TIMIT phoneme recognition by fine-tuning the pre-trained models on the labeled TIMIT training data. We fine-tune as for the 10 hour subset of Libri-light but do not use a language model. Table 3 shows that our approach can achieve a new state of the art on this dataset, reducing PER by a relative 23%/29% over the next best result on the dev/test sets. Appendix D shows an analysis of how the discrete latent speech representations related to phonemes. Other recent work on pre-training which evaluates on TIMIT includes [47] who solve multiple tasks to learn good representations of speech.
items.zh: 接下来，我们通过在标记的 TIMIT 训练数据上微调预训练模型来评估 TIMIT 音素识别的准确性。我们像对 Libri-light 的 10 小时子集一样进行微调，但不使用语言模型。表 3 显示我们的方法可以在该数据集上实现新的最先进水平，在开发/测试集上相对于次优结果将 PER 相对降低 23%/29%。附录 D 分析了离散潜在语音表示与音素的关系。其他在 TIMIT 上评估的预训练近期工作包括 [47]，他们解决多个任务以学习良好的语音表示。

items.en: A difference to previous work [5, 4] is that we quantize the latent audio representations only for the contrastive loss, i.e., when latents are used as targets, but not when the latents are input to the Transformer network. We motivate this choice by an ablating for which we adopt a reduced training setup to increase experimental turn around: we pre-train Base on LS-960 for 250k updates with masking probability p=0.075𝑝0.075p=0.075, fine-tune on train-10h for 60k updates on a single GPU with 640k samples per batch, or 40 sec of speech audio. We report the average WER and standard deviation on the concatenation of dev-clean and dev-other (dev PER) for three seeds of fine-tuning.
items.zh: 与先前工作 [5, 4] 的一个区别是，我们仅对对比损失量化潜在音频表示，即当潜在表示用作目标时，而不是当潜在表示输入到 Transformer 网络时。我们通过消融实验来证明这一选择的合理性，为此我们采用简化的训练设置以加快实验周转：我们在 LS-960 上预训练 Base 250k 更新，掩码概率 p=0.075，在 train-10h 上微调 60k 更新，使用单个 GPU，每批 640k 样本（即 40 秒语音音频）。我们报告 dev-clean 和 dev-other 连接（dev PER）上三个微调种子的平均 WER 和标准差。

items.en: We presented wav2vec 2.0, a framework for self-supervised learning of speech representations which masks latent representations of the raw waveform and solves a contrastive task over quantized speech representations. Our experiments show the large potential of pre-training on unlabeled data for speech processing: when using only 10 minutes of labeled training data, or 48 recordings of 12.5 seconds on average, we achieve a WER of 4.8/8.2 on test-clean/other of Librispeech.
items.zh: 我们提出了 wav2vec 2.0，一个用于自监督学习语音表示的框架，该框架对原始波形的潜在表示进行掩码，并解决一个关于量化语音表示的对比任务。我们的实验展示了在未标注数据上进行预训练在语音处理中的巨大潜力：仅使用 10 分钟的标注训练数据（平均 48 段 12.5 秒的录音），我们在 Librispeech 的 test-clean/other 上分别达到了 4.8/8.2 的词错误率（WER）。

items.en: Our model achieves results which achieve a new state of the art on the full Librispeech benchmark for noisy speech. On the clean 100 hour Librispeech setup, wav2vec 2.0 outperforms the previous best result while using 100 times less labeled data. The approach is also effective when large amounts of labeled data are available. We expect performance gains by switching to a seq2seq architecture and a word piece vocabulary.
items.zh: 我们的模型在完整的 Librispeech 噪声语音基准测试中取得了新的最佳结果。在干净的 100 小时 Librispeech 设置下，wav2vec 2.0 在使用的标注数据量少 100 倍的情况下，超越了之前的最佳结果。该方法在大量标注数据可用时也同样有效。我们预计通过切换到 seq2seq 架构和词片词汇表，性能将进一步提升。

items.en: There are around 7,000 languages in the world and many more dialects. However, for most of them no speech recognition technology exists since current systems require hundreds or thousands of hours of labeled data which is hard to collect for most languages. We have shown that speech recognition models can be built with very small amounts of annotated data at very good accuracy. We hope our work will make speech recognition technology more broadly available to many more languages and dialects.
items.zh: 世界上大约有 7000 种语言和更多方言。然而，对于大多数语言，目前不存在语音识别技术，因为现有系统需要数百或数千小时的标注数据，这对大多数语言来说难以收集。我们已经证明，使用极少量的标注数据就能以非常高的准确率构建语音识别模型。我们希望我们的工作能让语音识别技术更广泛地应用于更多语言和方言。

items.en: We thank Tatiana Likhomanenko and Qiantong Xu for helpful discussion and their help with wav2letter integration.
items.zh: 我们感谢 Tatiana Likhomanenko 和 Qiantong Xu 的有益讨论以及他们在 wav2letter 集成方面的帮助。
